description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline for a Windows .NET project using PowerShell commands. Define three stages: build, test, and deploy. In the build stage add a build_job that runs '& "$env:NUGET_PATH" restore' then '& "$env:MSBUILD_PATH" /p:Configuration=Release', produces artifacts expiring in 1 week for $env:EXE_RELEASE_FOLDER\YourApp.exe, $env:MSI_RELEASE_FOLDER\YourApp Setup.msi and $env:TEST_FOLDER\, and runs only on tags. In the test stage add a test_job that depends on build_job, runs '& "$env:NUNIT_PATH" ".\$env:TEST_FOLDER\Tests.dll"', always uploads .\TestResult.xml as an artifact expiring in 1 week, and runs only on tags. In the deploy stage add a deploy_job that depends on build_job and test_job, runs only on tags, extracts the latest commit subject with 'git log -1 --pretty=%s', builds a deploy folder path using $env:DEPLOY_FOLDER, $CI_BUILD_TAG and the commit subject, then copies the built exe, msi and TestResult.xml to that folder using xcopy /y ".\$env:EXE_RELEASE_FOLDER\YourApp.exe" "$deployFolder", xcopy /y ".\$env:MSI_RELEASE_FOLDER\YourApp Setup.msi" "$deployFolder", and xcopy /y ".\TestResult.xml" "$deployFolder".
